# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure Virtual Machines running our web application. Include OS and data disks with default settings for quick deployment. Focus on getting VMs running - we'll add disk encryption and security features in phase 2 after validating the architecture.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

# ARM template to create an Azure Virtual Machine (VM) using parameters defined as input variables
parameters:
    vmName:
        type: string # required, defines name for VMs that will be created by this solution 
  
    resourceGroupName:
       defaultValue: 'your-resourcegroup'      # optional parameter; sets the group of resources to deploy in (if omitted it is set from the subscription)
    
variables:
    vmSize: 'Standard_D2s_v3'                # VM size, e.g., "Small", "Medium" or “Large". This can be customized by changing values here according requirement
  
    location: resourceGroup().location       # Location for all resources - Default is the subscription’s default region and should not change in this template because that requires a re-deployment of entire solution. You could also use Azure Data Center, West Europe etc...  you can customize it based on your requirement.
  
    adminUsername: 'youradminuser'            # required parameter; the user name to log into VM via SSH and RDP after deployment by this template is executed as part of a larger pre-deployment workflow in Azure DevOps or other similar environment    
 
resources:                     
 - Deploy either OS disk, data disks (1x are created for quick creation) then the web app itself. If desired to have an image with all dependencies installed e.g., IIS server and MSSQL Database will also be included in this template but might not cover as much usage since they can take a lot of space
   - apiVersion: '2019-03-01'  # Use the latest API version for our resources, required by Azure resource providers. See https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/templates/quickstart-create-vm-arm?tabs=bicep
     type: 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines'  # Resource provider namespace and type (Microsoft.Compute for VMs)   should be consistent with your actual resourceprovider connection in the subscription
     
    - location: localAdminArea()        # Location needs to match Azure Data Center or West Europe, not changed after creation as they are pre-configured by Microsoft on an area level  and cannot change. You can customize it based upon requirement   but must also be consistent with any other required customizations in that region
      name: '${concat(parameters('vmName'),'_osDisk')} # Define your OS Disk Name as the combined of VM name + '_OSdisk'. This is to avoid conflict when creating more than one disk on this template   (like 2 disks) you can also define it dynamically like parameters()
      newServicePrincipal: true         # if 'true', a service principal will be created for deploying resources. If set false, the existing user account from your profile is used and rights are inherited by default subscription or resource groups that this template deploys into   (by setting scope to managementGroup / subscriptions)
      clientConnectionTimeout: 60        # The timeout in seconds of service connection for SSH here we want it as high but not overly critical. If the deployment take more than time, you could handle by retrying and canceling early if needed e.g., with azure devops or similar CI/CD tools
      userName: 'youradminuser'         # (Required) The name of your admin account in Azure AD to use for SSH logins on the VMs created as part of this deployment  you can change it based upon requirement and customize accordingly.     but must be same with above mentioned parameter   if not set, a blank user is used
      password: 'yourpassword'         # (Required) The passphrase or secret key to use for SSH logins on the VMs created as part of this deployment you can change it based upon requirement and customize accordingly.    but must be same with above mentioned parameter   if not set, a blank user is used
      encryptionEnabled: true            # If 'true', enable disk level security features e.g., 2-key OS-level backup or at least one extra data disks to add further protection on existing backups (like more than the default os and application logs). You can also customize it based upon requirement but must be set as per your requirements
      imageReference: 'yourimagereference' # This parameter is mandatory, when you are not deploying in a custom VM - should contain URI of an Azure resource that will act to fulfill this OS request. For example Microsoft has Image Builder service which could help construct images for ARM templates using the same approach as described here 
      diskEncryptionSetId: '/subscriptions/your-id/resourceGroups/{ResourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.ManagedIdentity/userAssignedIdentities/myDiskEncryptionSet' # If 'true', create a new user assigned managed identity and use it for encryption, default '' (an empty string)
      dataDisks: 2                        # the number of additional disks to be created as part of this template   must match with OS disk size according requirement. if not set will include only one osDisk in case when imageReference is defined otherwise two or more disKs are added on top for storing default logs, application files and temporary storage
     tags: 'your-tags'                      # any additional tag assignments to be done during the template deployment (like setting version number) must not start with @ as it can cause errors in ARM if used. customization also has its own rules so always have a clear idea of your requirements about this parameter  you need provide correct values or leave empty
      virtualNetworkId: 'your-vnet'          # the ID for VNET where VM should be created must not start with @ as it can cause errors in ARM if used. customization also has its own rules so always have a clear idea of your requirements about this parameter  you need provide correct values or leave empty
      ipConfigurationName: 'your-config'     # the name for IP Configuration on which VM should be created must not start with @ as it can cause errors in ARM if used. customization also has its own rules so always have a clear idea of your requirements about this parameter  you need provide correct values or leave empty
      availabilitySetId: 'your-availabilityset' # the ID for Availability Set on which VM should be created must not start with @ as it can cause errors in ARM if used. customization also has its own rules so always have a clear idea of your requirements about this parameter  you need provide correct values or leave empty
      loadBalancerType: 'your-lbtype'       # Load balancing type, options are Application Gateway and Classic (must not start with @ as it can cause errors in ARM if used. customization also has its own rules so always have a clear idea of your requirements about this parameter  you need provide correct values or leave empty
      loadBalancerSku: 'your-lbsku'          # Load balancing SKU, options like Basic/Standard (must not start with @ as it can cause errors in ARM if used. customization also has its own rules so always have a clear idea of your requirements about this parameter  you need provide correct values or leave empty
